title: Inventory satellite fire-detection, ground PM2.5, source-apportionment, and respiratory health data sources for the Indo-Gangetic Plain
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates satellite fire-detection products (MODIS, VIIRS, GFED), ground PM2.5 monitors (CPCB, state boards), source-apportionment studies, and respiratory health-utilization data into usable, limited, and rejected categories.
inputs: MODIS and VIIRS fire-detection documentation from NASA FIRMS
GFED emissions methodology and data access documentation
CPCB National Air Quality Monitoring Programme station inventory
Published source-apportionment studies for Delhi and Indo-Gangetic Plain
Hospital respiratory admission data availability from state health departments
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, spatial resolution, temporal coverage, and documented limitations — particularly satellite detection gaps during cloudy periods.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering resolution, coverage, access conditions, and known detection gaps.

title: Validate satellite fire-detection products against CPCB ground-monitor PM2.5 during burning and non-burning periods
outcome: A validation report documenting the relationship between satellite fire counts and ground-level PM2.5 at available CPCB stations during October-November burning season versus non-burning baseline months, with explicit documentation of monitor sparsity in rural burning areas.
inputs: MODIS and VIIRS fire-count data for Punjab, Haryana, and UP
CPCB PM2.5 data for available stations in the Indo-Gangetic Plain
Administrative boundary data for burning districts
Weather data for cloud-cover correction of satellite detection gaps
expected_artifact: validation.md update with fire-PM2.5 correlation methodology and results.
validation_method: Replicator reruns the fire-PM2.5 validation on a different time period and confirms the correlation direction and magnitude hold, with explicit documentation of which CPCB stations were used and why rural coverage is insufficient.
done_condition: Validation report includes fire-count-to-PM2.5 correlation coefficients, seasonal comparison (burning vs. non-burning), station selection rationale, and explicit documentation of rural monitoring gaps.

title: Produce sub-national source-attribution analysis separating agricultural burning PM2.5 from non-burning sources
outcome: A source-attribution analysis for at least two Indo-Gangetic Plain states that estimates the agricultural-burning contribution to peak-season PM2.5 while documenting the methodology for separating burning from residential biomass, industrial, vehicular, and Diwali-period sources.
inputs: Non-burning PM2.5 baseline data from CPCB stations
Meteorological data for dispersion modeling context
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with source-attribution methodology and results table.
validation_method: Replicator reruns source-attribution with alternative confound assumptions and confirms the burning-attribution range is consistent with published studies within stated methodology differences.
done_condition: Source-attribution analysis includes explicit methodology, burning-attribution percentage range with confidence intervals, confound documentation for at least three non-burning sources, and comparison with published source-apportionment studies.

title: Red-team agricultural burning source-attribution overstatement and intervention misdirection risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how burning-attribution maps could mislead policy toward agricultural interventions while ignoring larger non-burning PM2.5 sources, or could be used punitively against smallholder farmers without addressing the economic constraints of residue management.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every source-attribution claim has documented limitations for non-burning confounds, satellite detection gaps, and methodology-dependent ranges. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every agricultural-burning health claim is either blocked, softened to analytic language with explicit confound caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match agricultural extension and pollution control board decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the source-attribution analysis would change any real decision for state pollution control boards, agricultural extension services, or farmer organizations without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for pollution control and agricultural extension audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that burning-focused interventions ignore larger PM2.5 sources or punish farmers without providing viable alternatives. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Punjab Pollution Control Board officer), named decision (e.g., Happy Seeder subsidy targeting), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for individual farmer penalties), and replicated field-reality review.
